title: sideritis arborescens subsp. lusitanica
intro: Sideritis arborescens subsp. lusitanica is a subspecies of the tree sideritis plant, also known as Iron Wort or White Clary, a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Sideritis arborescens subsp. lusitanica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and soothe digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its therapeutic properties.
